calendar.matters: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Final Inventory And Appriasal
Off Calendar Apr-21-2026 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Nov-18-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Stacy Marie Johnson, petitioner in pro per.

The matter was called for hearing on Amended Petition for Letters of Administration.

The Court asked Ms. Johnson whether she understood the Duties and Liabilities of Personal Representative previously filed. Ms. Johnson confirmed she had reviewed the document and understood the obligations, noting that she had the assistance of a third-party company. The Court questioned Ms. Johnson regarding several specific responsibilities, including opening a separate, FDIC-insured estate account using an EIN; providing notice to creditors; and refraining from making any distributions, even as sole beneficiary, without court approval. Ms. Johnson confirmed her understanding of each requirement.

Following discussion, the Court granted the petition for letters of administration. Stacy Marie Johnson is appointed Administrator with full IAEA authority. Bond is waived. Michael Kasolas is appointed as probate referee. The Court disclosed that the assigned probate referee is married to a member of the court's legal advisory staff. Hearing on status of filing final inventory and appraisal is set for April 21, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. Hearing for status on close of estate is set for June 8, 2027, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. Order signed in open court.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Oct-28-2025 Continued To Nov-18-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Oct-07-2025 Continued To Oct-28-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Stacy Marie Johnson, petitioner in Pro Per.

The matter was called for hearing on Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters of Administration with Will Annexed.

The Court noted that the will submitted was a typewritten document signed by the decedent but lacking the required witnesses under Probate Code Section 6110(c)(1), and the will did not qualify as an unwitnessed holographic will because it was typed rather than handwritten.

The Court advised Ms. Johnson of two available options: 1) denial of the petition to allow petitioner to file a new petition for letters of administration (instead of administrator with will annexed); or 2) continue the matter to allow petitioner to file an amended petition for letters of administration.

Ms. Johnson requested a continuance to allow time to figure out her next steps.

The Court continued the matter to October 28, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, to allow Ms. Johnson time to determine how to proceed and file any new or amended petition.

Judge: Cindee Mayfield; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)
